name: Pretzels
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine yeast and 1/2 cup warm water in a measuring cup, stirring with a fork until dissolved.
2. In a bowl, combine 2 cups flour, salt, and sugar. Add the dissolved yeast and remaining 1/2 cup warm water. Beat vigorously with a whisk or spoon for 2 minutes.
3. Gradually add enough of the remaining flour, 1/4 cup at a time, to make a soft dough. Turn out onto a floured surface.
4. Knead until smooth and elastic, adding as little flour as necessary (about 8-10 minutes).
5. Place dough in an oiled bowl, cover with plastic wrap, and let rise until doubled in size.
6. Punch down the dough and divide into 12 equal portions. Roll each portion into an 18-20 inch long rope, slightly tapered and thinner at the ends.
7. Form into pretzel shapes. Place on parchment paper squares.
8. Cover with a towel and let rest for 20-30 minutes.
9. Bring a large pot of water to a boil with 6 cups boiling water, 1/3 cup baking soda, and 1/3 cup salt.
10. Gently drop 3 or 4 pretzels into the simmering water, parchment side up. Remove parchment. Poach each side for 2 minutes, then turn and poach the other side for another 2 minutes.
11. Using a slotted spatula, remove pretzels and place on a towel to cool.
12. Brush with an egg white wash (1 egg white mixed with 1 tablespoon water) and sprinkle with coarse salt.
13. Bake on greased, foil-lined baking sheets at 375°F for 30 minutes. Remove from the baking sheet and cool on wire racks.
These freeze very well.
